english: nas rnyed (nas rnyed) where did you get that? Dzl., also: whence shall I get it? Dzl.; mi rnyéd-du mi rún-ńo (mi rnyed du mi rung ngo) it must be got or procured by all means Dzl.; ńas rnyed (ngas rnyed) I obtain; rnyéd-par dká-ba (rnyed par dka' ba) difficult to be obtained, found, or met with, frq.; sdug-bsńal dań bsdós-te čos rnyéd-pa (sdug bsngal dang bsdos te chos rnyed pa) to purchase the acquisition of religion by suffering tortures Dzl.; zas dań skom ma rnyéd-de (zas dang skom ma rnyed de) having nothing to eat or to drink, frq.; don rnyéd-pa (don rnyed pa) v. don (don); da ni ré-ba rnyéd-do (da ni re ba rnyed do) now my hopes are realized Dzl.; gri rnyéd-pas (gri rnyed pas) as he found a knife Dzl.; skabs rnyéd-pa (skabs rnyed pa) to find an opportunity Dzl.; btsál-na yań ma rnyéd-de (btsal na yang ma rnyed de) not finding it in spite of every search Dzl., (W. *tob-če* (tob ce)). II. sbst. profit, gain, acquisition, property, goods. rnyéd-pa máń-po rnyed-pa (rnyed pa mang po rnyed pa) (or *fób-če* (phob ce)) to gain much profit: bdag rnyéd-pa dań ldán-na mi dga (bdag rnyed pa dang ldan na mi dga) if I have got some earnings, he envies me for them; often in conjunction with grágs-pa (grags pa) and similar expressions: riches and honour. - rnyed sdú-ba (rnyed sdu ba), rnyéd-pa ṕróg-pa (rnyed pa phrog pa) Sch.: to make booty, to plunder. - rnyed-bkúr (rnyed bkur) Lex., prob. riches and honour. - rnyed-nor (rnyed nor) v. fob-nór (phob nor).

(cf. nyóg-pa (nyog pa)) vb., pf. brnyogs (brnyogs), fut. brnyog (brnyog), 1. to trouble, to stir up (s.; also adj.: thick, turbid. *ču nyóg-pa* (chu nyog pa) W. - 2. to rub one's self, ká-ba-la (ka ba la) against a pillar Dzl. (snyóg-pa (snyog pa)). - *nyóg(-pa)-čan* (nyog (pa) can), *nyóg-po* (nyog po) C., troubled, turbid, dirty; rnyóg-pa méd-pa (rnyog pa med pa) clear, limpid, mtso (mtso) Wdn. - rnyóg-ma (rnyog ma) dirty, muddy water; mud, mire. rnyóg-ma-čan (rnyog ma can) muddy, miry.
Lt.? rnyogs-fsád (rnyogs tshad) a disease Med.

seems to be the same as rnyi (rnyi) Lexx.; rnyóń-ba (rnyong ba), pf. brnyońs (brnyongs), fut. brnyoń (brnyong) 1. Cs.: 'to ensnare, entrap'. 2. Sch.: 'to stretch out. I met with rnyoń (rnyong) in the following expressions, not satisfactorily to be explained either by Cs. or by Sch.: rkáń-pa rnyoń (rkang pa rnyong) Lex.; dku ma rnyońs (dku ma rnyongs) Lex.; bus rnyóń-ba (bus rnyong ba) S.g.; frq.; gyal-rnyóńs (gyal rnyongs) S.g.; mgul-rgyab zug dań rnyoń (mgul rgyab zug dang rnyong) S.g.; rnyońs-fsád (rnyongs tshad) Mng.
col. for snyég-pa (snyeg pa); also in Mil.
Lex. w.e.; C. = dbyańs (dbyangs) music, harmony.

malicious or false accusation or imputation, snyad ˓dzúg-pa (snyad 'dzug pa) (W. *tsug-če* (tsug ce)) to bring in an action against, to prosecute; *nyad dú-če* (nyad du ce) W., *nye' kó wa* (nye' ko wa) C. id., esp. to irritate, to provoke another, by accusations; snyad ˓dágs-pa (snyad 'dags pa) id. Glr.; snyad ˓dág-pa (snyad 'dag pa), W. *dág-če* (dag ce) to clear one's self of an accusation, to refute it; snyad byéd-pa (snyad byed pa) c.dat. to use as a pretence or pretext Glr.; *nye' čó'* (nye' cho') (or *če'* (che')) *táń-wa* (tang wa) C., *nyad-sé tág-če* (nyad se tag ce) Ld. to weary another by too great punctiliousness, ni f.; *nor-nyád čó-če* (nor nyad cho ce) W. to extort money by false accusations, la (la) from; snyad méd-par (snyad med par) without cause, pretence, or provocation Thgy.; *nyád-zer-čan* (nyad zer can) W. one that makes false accusations.

pf. and fut. bsnyad (bsnyad), imp. snyod (snyod), to relate, to report, e.g. lo-rgyús (lo rgyus) a story Pth., rmí-lam (rmi lam) a dream Dzl.; gtam snyád-pa (gtam snyad pa) 1. to speak, state, inform, give notice (W. *hun táń-če* (hun tang ce)). 2. Cs.: 'to rehearse'(?).

1. resp. the ear, rgyál-poı snyán-du tos (rgyal po'i snyan du tos) it came to the king's hearing Glr.; snyán-du žus (snyan du zhus) or brjod (brjod) they told or informed him Pth.; snyán-du zuń (snyan du zung) listen, pay attention, give ear to! Pth.; snyán-du ṕul (snyan du phul) they sang to him or before him (lit. they made him hear) Mil. (cf. sub snyán-pa (snyan pa)); snyán-(gyis) gsán-pa (snyan (gyis) gsan pa) to hear Mil.; snyan-gsán bébs-pa (snyan gsan bebs pa) to give ear to one, to hear one Cs.; *nyéń-žu bul-wa* (nyeng zhu bul wa) C. to address a superior, to apply to him; snyan-kúń (snyan kung) the ear-hole; snyan-dbáń (snyan dbang) the organ of hearing Cs.; snyan-šál (snyan shal) the lap or tip of the ear Cs.; snyan-ṕrá žú-ba (snyan phra zhu ba) to slander, mi mí-la (mi mi la) to calumniate one person to another. - 2. = ynyan (ynyan) argali.
1. sbst. renown, glory, fame, praise, rumour, kyód-kyí (kyod kyi)
